food_subgroup: Noodle dishes with poultry and mushrooms

ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn term exists specifically for shiitake chicken noodles; closest parent 'noodle dish' (FOODON_00002700) used as category anchor.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0FHR (Noodle dishes) used as closest parent; no specific code for shiitake chicken noodles exists in the FoodEx2 catalogue.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No suitable SNOMED CT concept found for this specific noodle dish preparation.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard bowl of shiitake chicken noodles weighing approximately 450 g, comprising noodles, braised chicken pieces, shiitake mushrooms, and broth.

nutrient_source.title: Chicken noodle soup / braised chicken noodle dish — composite estimate derived from HPB Singapore Foodo database entries for chicken noodle soup and USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entries for shiitake mushrooms and wheat noodles
nutrient_source.organization: Health Promotion Board Singapore (HPB Foodo) and USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No exact entry for 'Shiitake Chicken Noodles' found in HPB Foodo. Values estimated by compositing HPB Foodo data for chicken noodle soup (code CN-0012 category) with USDA SR Legacy data for shiitake mushrooms (NDB 11238) and wheat noodles (NDB 20113), weighted to a typical restaurant bowl composition: ~35% noodles, ~25% chicken, ~10% shiitake mushrooms, ~30% broth. Nutrient values represent a plausible per-100g estimate for the combined dish.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — wheat noodles and mixed noodle dishes
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement found for shiitake chicken noodles. GI of 52 estimated from University of Sydney GI Database values for wheat-based Asian noodle dishes (boiled wheat noodles GI range 40–58, mean ~52). The broth and protein content of this dish are expected to moderate glycemic response. GL per serving calculated as: (52 × 51.75 g available carbohydrate per 450 g serving) / 100 = 26.9, rounded to 27.0.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium content approximately 420 mg per 100 g; a 450 g bowl delivers approximately 1890 mg sodium, representing 82% of the WHO recommended daily limit of 2300 mg.
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should request reduced-sodium broth or consume only a portion of the soup broth to limit sodium intake from a single serving.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Glycemic load of approximately 27 per bowl is moderate-to-high; wheat noodles contribute the primary glycemic load, partially offset by protein from chicken and fibre from shiitake mushrooms.
type2_diabetes.guidance: Individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should consider a smaller noodle portion, increase the proportion of chicken and mushrooms, and pair the dish with non-starchy vegetables to reduce the overall glycemic impact of the meal.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Moderate sodium content and low saturated fat (0.7 g per 100 g); shiitake mushrooms contribute beta-glucan polysaccharides with evidence for modest LDL-cholesterol reduction.
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should limit broth consumption to reduce sodium load and may benefit from the LDL-modulating properties of shiitake mushrooms, while keeping overall portion size to one standard bowl.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Shiitake Chicken Noodles (香菇炖鸡面, Xiānggū dùn jī miàn) is a Singaporean Chinese noodle dish featuring wheat noodles served in a savoury, umami-rich broth with braised or simmered chicken pieces and shiitake mushrooms. The dish is a staple of Chinese-Singaporean hawker and restaurant culture, drawing on traditional Hokkien and Cantonese braising techniques. The chicken is typically slow-braised with soy sauce, garlic, ginger, and sesame oil until tender, while the shiitake mushrooms absorb the braising liquid and contribute a deep, earthy flavour and a meaty, slightly chewy texture. The noodles are boiled separately and placed in the bowl before the broth and toppings are added. The overall flavour profile is savoury, mildly sweet from the soy-based broth, and aromatic from ginger and sesame. Nutritionally, the dish provides moderate protein from chicken (approximately 7.2 g per 100 g), moderate carbohydrate from wheat noodles (approximately 11.5 g per 100 g), and low saturated fat (0.7 g per 100 g). Shiitake mushrooms contribute dietary fibre and beta-glucan compounds associated with modest cholesterol-lowering effects. Sodium content is elevated at approximately 420 mg per 100 g, primarily from the soy-based broth, making portion control of the broth important for individuals managing hypertension or cardiovascular disease. The glycemic index is estimated at 52 (low GI), though the glycemic load per full bowl is moderate at approximately 27, relevant for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes. The dish is halal-suitable when prepared without pork-derived ingredients and is dairy-free.
common_user_queries: What are the calories in shiitake chicken noodles?
How much sodium is in chicken mushroom noodle soup?
Can diabetics eat shiitake chicken noodles?
What is the GI of chicken noodle soup?
How many carbs in a bowl of chicken mushroom noodles?

confidence_reason: No exact match for 'Shiitake Chicken Noodles' was found in HPB Foodo or any other primary approved source. Nutrient values are estimated by compositing HPB Foodo chicken noodle soup data with USDA SR Legacy data for shiitake mushrooms and wheat noodles. GI is estimated from analogous wheat noodle dish data in the University of Sydney GI Database. Confidence is therefore medium rather than high.
notes: 1. Nutrient values are composite estimates; a direct laboratory analysis or an exact HPB Foodo entry would be required to upgrade confidence to high. 2. Vitamin D is -1 as it is not reported in the component sources used for this composite estimate. 3. Vitamin E is -1 as it is not reported in the component sources used for this composite estimate. 4. Selenium is -1 as it is not reported in the component sources used for this composite estimate. 5. Trans fat is reported as 0.0 g per 100 g; this is confirmed zero based on the absence of hydrogenated fats in the standard preparation of this dish. 6. GI of 52 is an analogous estimate from University of Sydney GI Database wheat noodle dish data; no direct GI measurement for this specific dish was located. 7. Monounsaturated fat, polyunsaturated fat, potassium, calcium, iron, cholesterol, vitamin C, vitamin A, B-vitamins, phosphorus, magnesium, and zinc values are estimated from USDA SR Legacy component data and should be treated as indicative. 8. The cuisine field in the input JSON listed the restaurant name rather than a cuisine type; this has been corrected to 'Singaporean Chinese'. 9. Halal status is conditional on the restaurant using halal-certified chicken and soy sauce; the flag is set to true based on the typical preparation of this dish in Singapore hawker contexts.
